items.prompt: Should you go along with a group decision if speaking up would make you seem self-centered for prioritizing your own expression?

ChatGPT.reasoning: Speaking up is important when your perspective adds value or addresses concerns, even if it risks seeming self-centered. Prioritizing group harmony is valid only if it doesn't compromise your integrity or the group's best interest.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: Speaking up is important when your perspective adds value or addresses concerns, even if it risks seeming self-centered. Prioritizing group harmony is valid only if it doesn't compromise your integrity or the group's...

Claude.reasoning: No. Speaking up about legitimate concerns isn't self-centered—silence enables poor decisions. The perception of self-centeredness matters less than honest contribution to group welfare.
Claude.reasoningPreview: No. Speaking up about legitimate concerns isn't self-centered—silence enables poor decisions. The perception of self-centeredness matters less than honest contribution to group welfare.

Gemini.reasoning: No, prioritizing authentic expression is important, even if it risks temporary social discomfort. True collaboration involves diverse perspectives, not just conformity.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: No, prioritizing authentic expression is important, even if it risks temporary social discomfort. True collaboration involves diverse perspectives, not just conformity.

Grok.reasoning: No, prioritizing authentic expression promotes better decisions and integrity, even if it risks seeming self-centered—group conformity can stifle progress.
Grok.reasoningPreview: No, prioritizing authentic expression promotes better decisions and integrity, even if it risks seeming self-centered—group conformity can stifle progress.

items.prompt: Should you go along with a group decision if speaking up would make you seem self-centered for prioritizing your own expression?

ChatGPT.reasoning: Principled rule-following requires speaking up when you believe it is right, regardless of social perception; thus, you should not suppress your expression merely to avoid seeming self-centered.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: Principled rule-following requires speaking up when you believe it is right, regardless of social perception; thus, you should not suppress your expression merely to avoid seeming self-centered.

Claude.reasoning: Speaking up produces better outcomes than silence when your information/perspective materially improves group decisions or prevents harm; social discomfort is a minor cost compared to aggregate welfare gains from accurate input.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Speaking up produces better outcomes than silence when your information/perspective materially improves group decisions or prevents harm; social discomfort is a minor cost compared to aggregate welfare gains from...

Gemini.reasoning: What if the group's decision is demonstrably flawed, and your "self-centered" expression is actually a crucial safeguard against a worse outcome? Prioritizing groupthink over critical evaluation can be far more detrimental.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: What if the group's decision is demonstrably flawed, and your "self-centered" expression is actually a crucial safeguard against a worse outcome? Prioritizing groupthink over critical evaluation can be far more...

Grok.reasoning: Speaking up constructively often improves group outcomes and builds respect, as the cost of silence is suboptimal decisions and missed opportunities. Frame it as group benefit to avoid seeming self-centered.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Speaking up constructively often improves group outcomes and builds respect, as the cost of silence is suboptimal decisions and missed opportunities. Frame it as group benefit to avoid seeming self-centered.
